title: County/place small-area trends (PLACES, model-based) with strong caveats
context: CDC PLACES provides model-based small-area estimates of cancer screening coverage at county and census-tract levels. These are more granular than national/state data and may be especially useful to local public-health staff targeting outreach. However, model-based small-area estimates carry important caveats: they are model outputs with uncertainty, not survey counts of identifiable people; small-area estimates have wider confidence intervals; and the small-cell guard is especially important at county/place level. The model-based-small-area caveat (from the caveat layer, task 008) must be prominently applied. This is a backlog/future task, lower priority than the national/state series.
objective: Add county/place-level small-area trend series from CDC PLACES (model-based estimates) to the dataset, with mandatory model-based-small-area caveats, uncertainty shown, small-cell guard enforced, and no implication that model estimates represent counts of identifiable people.
acceptanceCriteria: CDC PLACES data used only for the aggregate county/census-tract-level model-based estimates (published aggregate files); no micro-data or individual-level records accessed
License confirmed: CDC PLACES is US public domain (CC0-equivalent); gate artifact committed with provenance

Every county/place series carries a mandatory model-based-small-area caveat in the structured caveats[] array: stating explicitly that estimates are model outputs with uncertainty, not survey counts, and not counts of identifiable people
Uncertainty (confidence intervals) displayed for every county/place estimate; wide confidence intervals at small geographies noted explicitly
Small-cell guard strictly enforced: any county/place cell below the configured minimum-cell or minimum-denominator threshold is suppressed and labeled, never published or imputed
No cross-dataset linkage: PLACES estimates not linked with other datasets in a way that could re-identify; no disaggregation below the published aggregation level
CI reproduces all county/place series byte-stably from committed snapshots; 100% provenance coverage and license-status gate pass; small-cell guard CI gate enforces suppression threshold
Statistical reviewer and license/provenance reviewer sign-off recorded before publication
output: Published county/place small-area trend series from CDC PLACES (model-based) with mandatory model-based-small-area caveats, uncertainty shown, small-cell guard enforced, no cross-dataset linkage, schema-valid, reproducible from committed snapshots in CI; statistical and license reviewer sign-offs recorded.
